title: taskiq-core: task-based image quality on synthetic phantoms
description:
taskiq-core measures physical image quality (MTF, NPS, NEQ) and task performance (model-observer detectability d' and AUC) on the same synthetic phantoms, through one pipeline, in order to quantify how physical metrics transfer to task performance as acquisition conditions are swept.

All data are synthetic and generated analytically. Every estimator is validated against a closed-form ground truth rather than against a stored snapshot of its own output: the slanted-edge MTF is held to exp(-2π²σ²f²) for an analytically Gaussian-blurred edge, and the NPS normalisation is held to the exact identity ∫NPS = σ². Pure Python (numpy, scipy, scikit-image, matplotlib), deterministic under a seed, with no deep learning and no patient data.
